cards.front: What is super keyword used for in Java?
cards.back: The super keyword is used to access the constructor and methods of the parent class in a subclass.
It is a type of inheritance where a subclass of a child class inherits the properties of its parent class.

cards.front: What happens when we don't use the super keyword in a subclass?
cards.back: It creates a new copy of the parent class and its properties, resulting in code duplication.
It is a feature in Java that allows multiple constructors with the same name but different parameters.

cards.front: What is the purpose of using the 'this' keyword in a subclass?
cards.back: It is used to refer to the current object and its properties, and to call the constructors of the parent class.
